column1: # This database contains REAL vulnerability tests for authorized security assessments only
# Unauthorized access or use is strictly prohibited

column9: description
description
.git/config exposes remote origin URLs with embedded credentials.
column10: Remove .git directories from production web root.

column9: .git/HEAD reveals current branch and confirms git exposure.
column10: Block .git paths in web server config.

column9: .git/index downloads entire staging area metadata.
packed-refs exposes all branch and tag references.
Git logs reveal commit history author names and emails.
Subversion entries file reveals repository structure and revision info.
SVN working copy database leaks entire project structure.
Mercurial store manifest reveals repository file listings.
Apache server-status shows active connections and request details.
Apache server-info reveals module configuration and directives.
phpinfo() dumps all PHP configuration environment and headers.
info.php exposes PHP configuration including database credentials.
Default PHP test script may reveal server information.
Backup of wp-config.php exposes database credentials.
Editor backup of wp-config.php contains database passwords.
column10: Prevent editor backups in web root.

column9: Save file of wp-config exposes database credentials.
.env file exposes database credentials API keys and secrets.
.env.local overrides production settings with debug credentials.
.env.production exposes live credentials and tokens.
.env.backup contains plaintext secrets and API tokens.
column10: Store environment backups in secure storage.

column9: Rails database.yml exposes production DB credentials.
Rails secrets.yml exposes secret_key_base for session signing.
Rails credentials encrypted file is downloadable.
Symfony parameters.yml exposes database credentials.
Magento env.php exposes database and cache credentials.
Joomla configuration.php reveals database credentials.
Drupal settings.php reveals database credentials and salt.
Drupal default.settings.php can reveal configuration patterns.
column10: Remove default settings files after install.

column9: Laravel logs contain SQL queries with bound parameters.
Laravel debug logs contain stack traces with env values.
System logs contain authentication attempts and errors.
System auth logs contain failed login IPs and usernames.
column10: Protect auth logs from web access.

column9: Apache access logs reveal query parameters and tokens.
Apache error logs may contain script paths and stack traces.
column10: Move Apache logs to non-web directory.

column9: Nginx access logs expose URL parameters including auth tokens.
column10: Store Nginx logs outside web root.

column9: Nginx error logs may reveal application paths and config.
MySQL error logs contain query errors and potential data.
Slow query log reveals database queries with parameters.
column10: Store database logs outside web root.

column9: Full database dump contains all tables including user credentials.
column10: Never store database dumps in web root.

column9: Database SQL dump contains table structures and data.
column10: Store backups in secure offsite location.

column9: SQL export of database may include password hashes.
Backup SQL file contains complete database with PII.
MySQL dump contains user tables with credential hashes.
PostgreSQL dump reveals schema and table contents.
MongoDB BSON dump contains all collections and documents.
JSON data dump contains user PII and credentials.
column10: Never export data to world-readable locations.

column9: CSV export of users exposes emails names and roles.
column10: Authenticate and audit all data exports.

column9: Customer CSV export contains address phone and order history.
column10: Require admin approval for data exports.

column9: Order data export reveals customer purchase history.
Error logging API stores stack traces with query data.
Debug log endpoint reveals application internals.
Access log API exposes IPs user agents and paths.
column10: Restrict log API access to internal.

column9: Audit log API reveals user actions and timestamps.
Performance logs reveal slow endpoints and query times.
column10: Aggregate perf data without identifying info.

column9: PII export endpoint leaks personally identifiable information.
Contact export reveals user address books.
Message export reveals private conversation content.
Payment export leaks card tokens and transaction history.
column10: Mask payment method details in exports.

column9: Address export reveals customer physical locations.
column10: Require business justification for address exports.

column9: Phone number export enables mass contact harvesting.
Batch user endpoint returns user profiles in bulk.
column10: Limit batch size and require admin scope.

column9: User enumeration via email existence check allows data gathering.
column10: Return consistent response for existing and non-existing users.

column9: User search reveals registered users and profile info.
column10: Restrict search to authenticated users only.

column9: Email list endpoint enables bulk email harvesting.
column10: Never expose email lists via API.

column9: User CSV export contains all profile fields.
column10: Audit and limit user data exports.

column9: Admin user list reveals all registered accounts.
column10: Require elevated role for user listing.

column9: Admin audit log exposes sensitive admin actions.
column10: Mask sensitive fields in audit log.

column9: Admin config exposes feature flags and system settings.
Admin secrets endpoint exposes API keys and tokens.
column10: Never expose raw secrets via API.

column9: Credential listing reveals service account passwords.
API key listing exposes all application keys.
column10: Mask API keys in list responses.

column9: Token listing reveals active session tokens.
Permission matrix reveals role hierarchy and access levels.
column10: Restrict permission view to security admin.

column9: Audit trail reveals exactly who changed what.
column10: Protect audit log integrity via append-only.

column9: User analytics reveal DAU MAU and retention metrics.
Revenue analytics expose financial performance.
Conversion analytics reveal business performance indicators.
Geographic analytics reveal user location distribution.
Device analytics reveal user agent and platform data.
column10: Aggregate device data with minimum counts.

column9: Referrer analytics reveal business partner traffic.
Event analytics expose user interaction patterns.
Funnel analytics reveal conversion drop-off points.
column10: Restrict funnel analysis to marketing team.

column9: Cohort analysis reveals user retention patterns.
Session analytics expose average session duration and depth.
Pageview analytics leak unpopular internal pages.
Analytics export contains full raw event data.
Raw event data access reveals individual user behavior.
column10: Require strict justification for raw data.

column9: AB test assignments reveal experiment details.
Feature flags reveal upcoming features and strategies.
Rollout percentages reveal feature release strategy.
Canary deployment info reveals testing strategy.
Experiment configs reveal user segmentation logic.
Personalization endpoint returns rich user profile.
Recommendation history reveals user browsing patterns.
Search history reveals user intent and interests.
Browsing history reveals page-level user activity.
Location history reveals user physical movement patterns.
column10: Require explicit consent for location data.

column9: Purchase history reveals spending habits and payment methods.
Communication log reveals emails calls and messages.
Support tickets reveal user issues and account details.
column10: Sanitize ticket data in API responses.
